title: rubus hirtus subsp. hercynicus
intro: Rubus hirtus subsp. hercynicus, also known as the Hercynian bramble or Hercynian raspberry, is a subspecies of the bramble plant (Rubus hirtus) native to Central and Eastern Europe. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Rubus hirtus subsp. hercynicus include reducing inflammation and pain, as well as supporting immune function and digestive health. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and can also be used in infusions and decoctions to harness its medicinal properties.
